subsystems.name: Atlas
subsystems.tagline: Research, persisted — structured views, not prose.
summary: Atlas runs scheduled research cycles across a configurable universe — equities, crypto, futures — pulling from open data sources (FRED, Treasury, CoinGecko, SEC/EDGAR, FX) on daily-delta, weekly-baseline, and monthly-rollup cadences.
Every cycle writes structured views to a shared store (Supabase by default), versioned and timestamped, so Hermes and Kairos re-use the same artifacts a human can audit.

subsystems.name: Hermes
summary: Hermes translates Atlas research into allocations. Each signal is timestamped, attributed to the views that produced it, and fully replayable — by you, by risk, or by your future self.
Signals carry their provenance, so any decision can be reconstructed from the research that drove it.

subsystems.name: Kairos
subsystems.tagline: Execution, gated. Strategies climb a ladder.
summary: Kairos executes Hermes signals on a NautilusTrader core. Strategies climb a ladder — backtest → paper → loopback → live — and each rung is a human gate.
Loopback-only by default: nothing consequential reaches a live venue until someone explicitly flips the gate, and every transition is audited.
